new_classes.label_en: Plum, Pear or Cherry Brandy in containers > 2 L (CN 2208 90 38)
new_classes.label_de: Pflaumen-, Birnen- oder Kirschbrand in Behältnissen > 2 L (KN 2208 90 38)
new_classes.definition_en: Spirituous beverage obtained exclusively by fermenting and distilling plum, pear or cherry musts (excluding liqueurs), presented in containers holding more than 2 litres. Corresponds to CN subheading 2208 90 38.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 90 38: (1) the product is a spirituous beverage obtained by fermenting and distilling stone fruit (plum, pear, or cherry) musts — modelled via the StoneFruitMashFermentationDistillationProcess and StoneFruitMash part restrictions; (2) it is presented in containers holding more than 2 litres — modelled via the LargeContainer part restriction; (3) it excludes liqueurs — modelled via a complement restriction on Liqueur. The sibling class StoneFruitBrandyInSmallContainer (CN 2208 90 33, ≤ 2 L) is already in the TBox, making the large-container distinction clear. Coverage is slightly below 1.0 because the legal note cross-references the HS Explanatory Note to heading 0809 for the terms 'plums' and 'cherries', which provides botanical/taxonomic scope that cannot be captured within the current TBox vocabulary without introducing botanical classification classes that would go beyond the current ontology design. The 'exclusively' requirement (only fermentation + distillation of the named musts, no blending of other spirits) is partially but not fully expressible in OWL-DL without closed-world assumptions.
